start.speaker: SYSTEM
start.text: [i]Jalen's workshop. Datapads scattered across the table, glowing with corporate feeds, manifests, financial trails. He looks up when you enter. His expression is wrong—not fear, but something worse. Certainty.[/i]

jalen_greeting.speaker: Jalen
jalen_greeting.text: Avyanna. Good. You should hear this. [i][He gestures to a chair.][/i] Sit down.

jalen_reveal_start.speaker: Jalen
jalen_reveal_start.text: I've been tracking corporate filings. Cross-referencing Aurum Extraction's debt recovery claims with Compact medical research permits. [i][He turns a datapad toward you.][/i] Look at this.

data_display.speaker: SYSTEM
data_display.text: [i]The screen shows a web of connections: Aurum Extraction, three medical research fronts, a Debtor shell corporation, and—buried at the center—a single word. [b]Primordial.[/b][/i]

jalen_explanation_1.speaker: Jalen
jalen_explanation_1.text: Aurum wasn't mining aurum at Site K-9. They were excavating [b]Primordial relics[/b]. The ore was cover. The real operation was Lattice extraction.

jalen_explanation_2.speaker: Jalen
jalen_explanation_2.text: They found something down there. Something old. Pre-Synthetic. And when you touched it—[i][He looks at you.][/i]—you became their primary asset.

avyanna_processing.speaker: Avyanna
avyanna_processing.text: [i]Voice thin.[/i] They knew. They knew what it was.

jalen_confirmation.speaker: Jalen
jalen_confirmation.text: They knew. And they let you touch it anyway. [i][He swipes to another file.][/i] Here—medical recovery permits filed three days after we extracted you. [b]'Debt recovery and asset reclamation.'[/b]

conspiracy_depth.speaker: Jalen
conspiracy_depth.text: It gets worse. The Debtor shell corporation owns a majority stake in Aurum. They're not just extracting ore—they're extracting [b]Primordial fragments to study resistance to the Synthetic overlay[/b].

conspiracy_target.speaker: Jalen
conspiracy_target.text: You're not just a debt. You're a [b]research target[/b]. They want the shard. They want to know how it bonded. They want to replicate it.

elia_arrives.speaker: SYSTEM
elia_arrives.text: [i]The workshop door slides open. Elia steps in, a datapad clutched in one hand. She's read something — her jaw is tight, eyes sharp with a cold fury you haven't seen before.[/i]

elia_reaction_01.speaker: Elia
elia_reaction_01.text: Jalen sent me a copy. [i][She sets the datapad down, carefully, like she's afraid she'll break it if she doesn't.][/i] I've seen corporate ugliness before. Wage theft, forced labor, AI exploitation. But this...

elia_reaction_02.speaker: Elia
elia_reaction_02.text: They [i]engineered[/i] the exposure. The safety protocols at K-9 were deliberately downgraded three weeks before your shift rotation. Jalen found the maintenance order — signed by someone in Aurum's 'research division.' They [i]wanted[/i] someone to touch it.

elia_reaction_03.speaker: Elia
elia_reaction_03.text: [i]Her voice drops, hard and quiet.[/i] You weren't an accident, Avyanna. You were an experiment. And they didn't even have the decency to tell you.

new_evidence_discovery.speaker: Jalen
new_evidence_discovery.text: [i][He pulls up one more file — this one encrypted, recently cracked.][/i] There's something else. I found a shipping manifest routed through a Compact waystation. Aurum was moving sealed crates labeled 'geological samples' to a black-site facility in the Reach Terminus sector.

new_evidence_02.speaker: Jalen
new_evidence_02.text: The crate weights don't match geological samples. They match [b]cryogenic containment units[/b]. Someone — or some [i]thing[/i] — was being transported alongside those relics. And the manifests were filed under your employee ID, Avyanna. Backdated.

new_evidence_03.speaker: Avyanna
new_evidence_03.text: [i]A cold knot in the stomach.[/i] They used my credentials. To cover their tracks. If anyone investigates, [i]I'm[/i] the one who signed for illegal cargo.

bee_analysis.speaker: SYSTEM
bee_analysis.text: {BEE:: Corporate data cross-reference complete | STATUS: Pattern confirmed | DETAIL: Aurum Extraction, Debtor Holdings, and Compact Medical Research Division 7 share 14 board members. Shell corporation nesting depth: 6 layers. Financial trail terminates at a classified Consortium defense account. This is not corporate greed — this is state-sponsored weapons research using Primordial artifacts as substrate.}

jalen_bee_react.speaker: Jalen
jalen_bee_react.text: [i][Reading the BEE output, his face goes pale.][/i] Consortium defense. This goes higher than corporate. This is [i]government[/i].

player_choice.speaker: SYSTEM
player_choice.text: [i]The weight of it settles. You're not free. You're fugitive property carrying contraband. The choice is how you respond.[/i]

choices.text: [Caution] We hide. Stay off grids. Don't give them a target.
[Aggression] We hit them first. Expose this publicly.
[Investigation] We need more data. Find their weak points.
[Defiance] They made me into a weapon. Fine. Let them regret it.

caution_response.speaker: Jalen
caution_response.text: [i]Nodding slowly.[/i] Smart. We stay mobile. Minimal station time. No Compact medical scans. I can scrub our trails—make us harder to track.

caution_vesper.speaker: Vesper
caution_vesper.text: [i]From the doorway—when did she arrive?[/i] Caution buys time. Time lets us prepare. I'll vet contracts more carefully—no clients with corporate ties.

caution_outcome.speaker: Jalen
caution_outcome.text: It's not a permanent solution. But it's survivable. [i][He looks at you.][/i] You okay with being a ghost?

aggression_response.speaker: Jalen
aggression_response.text: [i]Eyebrows raised.[/i] Bold. And risky. If we go public, they'll accelerate extraction. Legal enforcement, bounty hunters—everything.

aggression_vesper.speaker: Vesper
aggression_vesper.text: [i]Stepping into the room.[/i] But public exposure limits their options. They can't quietly disappear you if the galaxy is watching. [i][Beat.][/i] We'd need ironclad evidence. And a platform they can't suppress.

aggression_jalen.speaker: Jalen
aggression_jalen.text: I can build the case. Manifests, permits, financial trails. But once we file, there's no taking it back. [i][He looks at you.][/i] You'd be painting a target on your back.

aggression_choice.speaker: Avyanna
aggression_choice.text: [i]Jaw set.[/i] I've been a target since I touched that shard. At least this way, I'm fighting back.

investigation_response.speaker: Jalen
investigation_response.text: [i]A grim smile.[/i] My preferred approach. We need more than suspicion—we need proof. Names, dates, contracts. Everything that makes this stick.

investigation_vesper.speaker: Vesper
investigation_vesper.text: [i]Joining the table.[/i] Investigation takes time. And resources. But it gives us leverage—something to negotiate with if they come for us.

investigation_plan.speaker: Jalen
investigation_plan.text: I'll keep digging. Financial networks, shell corporations, research permits. We find the weak points—the places where exposure would cost them more than extraction.

investigation_vesper_addition.speaker: Vesper
investigation_vesper_addition.text: And I'll start building exit strategies. Safe houses, alternate identities, extraction routes. If this goes wrong, we need options. [i][She looks at you.][/i] You're not going back.

defiance_response.speaker: Avyanna
defiance_response.text: [i]Something shifts in her posture. Not anger — resolve. The kind forged in the same furnace as the shard in her chest.[/i] They made me into a weapon. They spent resources, manipulated safety protocols, ruined my life to create... this. Fine.

defiance_elia.speaker: Elia
defiance_elia.text: [i]A sharp look — measuring, then approving.[/i] That's not recklessness talking. That's someone who's done being afraid.

defiance_jalen.speaker: Jalen
defiance_jalen.text: [i]Quietly.[/i] Defiance without strategy is just noise. But defiance [i]with[/i] strategy... that's revolution. [i][He leans forward.][/i] Tell me what you need. I'll make it happen.
